title: Experten om AI-rallyt: "Läge att börja ta ett steg tillbaka"
summary_md:
# Experten om AI-rallyt: "Läge att börja ta ett steg tillbaka"

**Feed**: dagen-med-di  
**Published**: 2026-05-13  
**Market Sentiment**: Negative

⏱️ **Time Horizon Key**: ST = <3 months | MT = 3-12 months | LT = >12 months

---

## Episode Thesis

The episode is a cautionary allocation discussion about the AI rally, with the expert arguing it is time to step back and examine fundamentals as memory-chip (RAM) prices and AI-related profits have spiked to record but likely cyclical levels. Drawing parallels to the 2000 dotcom bubble, the hosts warn that record earnings driven by datacenter/AI buildout are unsustainable, that competition will erode prices, and that Emerging Markets are not a safe haven because their largest constituents ride the same semiconductor wave. The overall message is to diversify away from AI/memory exposure and favor sectors with better risk/reward.

---

## Universe Stocks

### ⛔ 000660 — SK Hynix | **AVOID** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Negative | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 60%

Memory-chip 'sweet spot' at the center of record but cyclical RAM-price spike; expert warns profits up hundreds of percent carry large downside.

#### Reasoning

The expert frames current memory profits as cyclical rather than structural, driven by a transient datacenter/AI bottleneck. History shows such demand spikes attract competition and price declines, so paying high valuations expecting prices to keep rising has not been profitable. SK Hynix, as the purest memory play, carries the most downside if the cycle turns.

#### Sell Triggers

- if RAM prices roll over from current peak
- if new competition enters and compresses memory margins
- if datacenter capex demand normalizes within 1-2 years

> *"Du har ju SK Hynix som verkligen är sweetspotten."*

---

### 🟠 TSM — Taiwan Semiconductor | **WATCH** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Mixed | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 50%

Largest EM constituent, affected by AI buildout; cited as reason EM is not the safe diversification haven it appears to be.

#### Reasoning

The expert cautions that Emerging Markets' strong run is driven by the same AI/semiconductor wave as the memory rally, not by independent fundamentals. TSMC, while not pure memory, is exposed to AI capex demand that the expert believes is cyclical, so EM exposure does not reduce the AI-concentration risk.

#### Sell Triggers

- if AI/datacenter capex demand peaks and declines
- if the AI buildout enters a downcycle like prior tech booms

> *"Dels är det Taiwanese Semiconductor. Det är inte exakt Raminne då, men det påverkas av den här A-utbyggnaden."*

---

### 🟠 005930 — Samsung Electronics | **WATCH** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Mixed | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 45%

Top-5 EM holding in the semiconductor sector; named among the AI/memory-exposed names making EM a risky 'safe haven'.

#### Reasoning

Grouped with TSMC and SK Hynix as the dominant EM names tied to the semiconductor/AI buildout. The expert's thesis is that these record profits are cyclical and that buying EM to diversify still leaves an investor exposed to the same memory/AI trend that he expects to mean-revert.

#### Sell Triggers

- if memory/semiconductor cycle turns down
- if AI capex demand normalizes

> *"Du har Samsung Electronics som är i den sektorn också i någon mening."*

---
